stories.headline: Committee to Protect Journalists calls on Ugandan authorities to immediately disclose whether they have detained journalist and activist
stories.summary: The Committee to Protect Journalists (CPJ) is calling on Ugandan authorities to disclose whether they have detained a journalist and activist who went missing after a radio show. The CPJ also urges authorities to lift restrictions on the Nation Media Group-Uganda, allowing them to cover parliament and presidential events freely. Additionally, the CPJ demands an investigation into the assault of journalist Juliet Kyarisiima and accountability for the perpetrators. The organization emphasizes the need for journalists to work without fear of arrest or violence. As of 2010, 145 journalists were jailed worldwide for their work, with current numbers being even higher. The CPJ is advocating for the Ugandan authorities to ensure the safe working conditions of journalists and to reveal the whereabouts of the missing journalist.

stories.headline: Iran Israel war live updates Spain has closed its airspace to US planes involved in the Iran war, says Defence Minister Margarita Robles
stories.summary: Spain has closed its airspace to US planes involved in the conflict with Iran, according to Defence Minister Margarita Robles. The minister stated that neither the use of military bases nor Spanish airspace is authorized for actions related to the war in Iran. Robles described the conflict as "profoundly illegal and profoundly unjust." This move underlines Spain's position as Europe's leading critic of the US-Israel war on Iran. The decision was reported by Spanish newspaper El Pais on Monday, with Robles confirming the news to reporters in Madrid. The closure of Spanish airspace to US aircraft involved in attacks on Iran is a significant escalation of Spain's opposition to the conflict. Robles reiterated that Spain's position on the matter is clear, emphasizing the country's disapproval of the war.

stories.headline: Dublin relatives worry for jailed man facing execution in Iran after alleged torture Peyvand Naimi, a member of a Bahá’í faith, is set to be executed by Iranian officials for what his family assert are false charges
stories.summary: Peyvand Naimi, a member of the Bahá'í faith, is facing execution in Iran after being arrested in January during protests. His family claims the charges against him are false and that he has been subjected to severe physical and psychological torture, including two mock hangings, while in custody. Naimi has been held in solitary confinement in Kerman Prison for over two months, with reports of torture aimed at extracting forced confessions. Human rights groups and Bahá'í organizations have raised concerns about his treatment and the threat of execution due to his religious beliefs. Iran has a high rate of executions, with the government claiming that they are only carried out after a lengthy judicial process, despite allegations of exaggerated numbers by human rights groups. Naimi's case has sparked concerns about the treatment of minority faiths in Iran.

stories.headline: The Saudi port of Yanbu is an escape hatch for some of the world’s oil The Houthis could slam it shut Earlier this month, Saudi Arabia began diverting millions of barrels of crude – ordinarily flowing through the blockaded Strait of Hormuz – to its Red Sea port
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia has begun diverting millions of barrels of crude to its Red Sea port of Yanbu, aiming to bypass the blockaded Strait of Hormuz. The move is an attempt to avoid the risk of Iranian attacks, which have slowed shipping in the Strait. State-owned oil company Aramco is rerouting crude exports to Yanbu, with the goal of increasing exports to 5 million barrels per day. A large number of tankers are waiting to collect oil from Yanbu as Riyadh ramps up efforts to use alternative routes. Despite these efforts, alternative routes still fall short of normal volumes through Hormuz. The East-West pipeline is being used to divert flows away from the disrupted Strait, highlighting the importance of Yanbu as a potential escape hatch for the world's oil. The port's increased activity comes amid heightened tensions in the region.

stories.headline: I Reviewed 6 Best Text-to-Speech Software for 2026 I evaluated 20+ tools to find the 6 best text-to-speech software for 2026, analyzing G2 reviews for ElevenLabs, Synthesia, Murf.ai, Veed, HeyGen, and more.
stories.summary: A review of 20+ text-to-speech software tools has identified the top 6 options for 2026. Murf.ai is recommended for lifelike multilingual voiceovers, offering professional controls and dubbing workflows for $19/month. VEED is best for collaborative AI video editing, with features like auto-subtitles and team collaboration for $9/month. HeyGen is ideal for photorealistic avatar customization and multilingual video translation. ElevenLabs is highly competitive for voice quality, with a large voice library, but at a higher cost. FishAudio-S1 ranks #1 in blind evaluations, followed closely by ElevenLabs. The top tools offer various features, including realistic voices, pronunciation control, and video editing capabilities, catering to different needs such as content creation, marketing, and training. Each tool has its unique strengths, with pricing starting from $9/month for VEED.

stories.headline: Bluesky's new AI tool Attie is already the most blocked account other than J.D. Vance More than 125,000 Bluesky users have blocked Attie in just a few days, sending a strong message from the user base.
stories.summary: Bluesky's new AI tool, Attie, has been blocked by over 125,000 users in just a few days. This makes Attie the second most blocked account on the platform, behind US Senator J.D. Vance. The significant number of blocks sends a strong message from the user base. Attie is an AI-powered feed-building application that was publicly released on Saturday. The blocking figure surpasses other notable accounts, including those of ICE and the White House. The rapid blocking of Attie suggests a negative reaction from Bluesky users to the new AI tool. Further details are being gathered, and updates will be provided as more information becomes available. The blocking of Attie is a notable development in Bluesky's integration of AI technology into its platform.

stories.headline: nubia Neo 5, Pro, GT 5G now official, priced in the Philippines nubia Philippines has officially launched the nubia Neo 5 series, bringing three 5G-ready smartphones to the local market.
stories.summary: Nubia Philippines has launched the nubia Neo 5 series, consisting of the nubia Neo 5, Neo 5 Pro, and Neo 5 GT. The Neo 5 GT 5G starts at ₱17,999, featuring a 6.8-inch OLED display with a 144Hz refresh rate and 1.5K resolution. The nubia Neo 5 Pro 5G is priced at ₱14,999 for the 12GB + 256GB model. The series is available in the Philippines, targeting competitive price-to-performance ratios. The devices are powered by Unisoc and Dimensity chips, catering to gaming needs. The launch was announced through a fan activation event at SM Megamall, with the series now officially available in the local market. Pricing and storage configurations vary across the models, offering options for different user preferences.

stories.headline: I recovered gigabytes of storage by running this free duplicate file finder Turns out my storage wasn’t full, it was just... duplicated to death.
stories.summary: A user recovered gigabytes of storage by utilizing a free duplicate file finder. Initially, they thought their storage was full, but it was actually filled with duplicated files. After running the duplicate file finder, they discovered 50GB of duplicate files and decided to pay for the pro version to delete them and merge folders. The process was successful, and they are now reorganizing their files. The user noted that simply deleting files doesn't immediately free up storage, as the storage blocks are only marked as "free to use" without actual data wiping. Restarting their Mac also freed up tens of GB of storage. The duplicate file finder compared file contents to identify duplicates, even if they had different names or locations.

stories.headline: Young Sooryavanshi hits 15-ball fifty as Rajasthan thumps Chennai by eight wickets in IPL
stories.summary: Rajasthan Royals thrashed Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets in the Indian Premier League (IPL). The match saw a remarkable performance from 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who hit a 15-ball fifty. Sooryavanshi was dropped on the first ball he faced but went on to score 52 off 17 balls, achieving the third fastest fifty in the IPL. His impressive innings powered Rajasthan's chase, leading the team to a convincing victory. The young sensation's performance has garnered significant attention, with many praising his talent and skill on the field. Sooryavanshi's 15-ball fifty is a notable achievement, especially considering his age and the competitive nature of the IPL.

stories.headline: MS Dhoni was absent, but CSK's collapse exposed deeper problems against RR
stories.summary: MS Dhoni was absent from CSK's match against Rajasthan Royals due to a calf strain, expected to keep him out for the first two weeks of the season. In his absence, CSK's innings lacked structure, exposing deeper problems. The team was depleted by injuries to Dhoni and Dewald Brevis, leading to franchise debuts for Matt Short and Kartik Sharma. CSK had spent a record INR 14.2 crore on Kartik in the December auction. The match, held on March 30 at Barsapara Cricket Stadium, saw CSK struggle, with the team at 94-9 in 16 overs, as the RR bowlers dominated. Dhoni's injury and the team's performance have raised concerns, with CSK likely to miss their captain for the first few games of the season. The team's lack of structure and depth was evident in their collapse against RR.

stories.headline: FC Barcelona Is Ready To Welcome Three Players Back To Action
stories.summary: FC Barcelona is set to welcome three players back to action following the international break. The team faces a challenging month ahead, starting with an away trip to Atletico Madrid and a home match against Athletic Club. According to reports, three key players are working to recover from injuries in time to join the squad. The players, whose identities have not been specified, are expected to be available for selection soon. This would be a significant boost for Hansi Flick's team as they prepare to face difficult opponents. The return of these players would add depth and strength to the squad, potentially impacting the outcome of upcoming matches. FC Barcelona will face Athletic Club at home, followed by other challenging fixtures, making the return of these players crucial for the team's success.

stories.headline: Here’s how to purchase tickets for 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games
stories.summary: The 2028 Los Angeles Olympic Games are scheduled to take place from July 14 to 30, 2028. To purchase tickets, fans must register for a ticket draw on the official website, LA28.org. Registration opens on January 14, allowing fans to enter a lottery for a randomly assigned time slot to purchase tickets. Interested individuals can head to LA28.org and follow the "REGISTER NOW" links to submit their entry. After registration, participants will be selected for the opportunity to buy tickets. The registration process is the first step to secure tickets for the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Olympic and Paralympic Games. By signing up, fans will be among the first to hear about official LA28 tickets and have a chance to attend the upcoming international multi-sport event. The ticket draw is the official method for fans to obtain tickets for the Games.

stories.headline: India strengthens naval fleet with triple warship handover; commissioning soon
stories.summary: India has strengthened its naval fleet with the handover of three warships from the Garden Reach Shipbuilders and Engineers (GRSE). The warships are expected to be commissioned soon, marking a significant milestone in the country's naval modernization efforts. This development comes as India aims to boost its naval capabilities, with a recent approval of an Rs 80 billion sustainment deal for its MH-60R helicopters. The Indian Navy has already taken delivery of 13 MH-60R helicopters out of a total order of 24. The country's defense minister has also overseen the commissioning of a new naval vessel at a Russian shipyard, highlighting India's efforts to enhance its naval presence and operations. The expansion of the naval fleet is part of India's broader strategy to strengthen its maritime security and defense capabilities.

stories.headline: Ashwini Bhide appointed first woman BMC Commissioner
stories.summary: Ashwini Bhide has been appointed as the first woman Commissioner of the Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC), following the retirement of incumbent chief Bhushan Gagrani. Bhide, a senior IAS officer, brings extensive administrative experience to the top civic post. Previously, she was appointed as the managing director of MMRC in 2015. Recently, she was tasked with overseeing the Corporation's contact tracing efforts and monitoring the control room to provide assistance to hospitals. With her new role, Bhide becomes the first woman to hold the position of Municipal Commissioner in the BMC's history. Her appointment is seen as a landmark administrative decision, and she is expected to bring her firm working style to the city's top civic post. Bhide's tenure as Commissioner begins after Gagrani's retirement on March 31.

stories.headline: Centre eyes new regulation to cover social media users
stories.summary: India's government plans to introduce a new IT rule allowing the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting to send takedown notices to individual social media users. The proposed regulation aims to tighten control over digital content, raising concerns over censorship. The government has reduced the time for social media companies to remove unlawful content from 36 hours to 3 hours. The new rule may also threaten the removal of safe harbor for social media platforms. The Ministry of Information and Broadcasting will have the authority to issue takedown notices to individual users for their social media content. The move is part of the government's efforts to regulate news and content on social media platforms, amid rising concerns over online content. The new regulation is expected to have significant implications for social media users and companies in India.

stories.headline: TVK president Vijay declares nearly ₹625 crore in assets and loans to family members
stories.summary: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) president C. Joseph Vijay has declared assets and loans worth nearly ₹625 crore in his election affidavit. He has ₹404.58 crore in movable assets and ₹220.15 crore in immovable assets. Additionally, he has declared personal loans to family members. His spouse, Sangeetha, has declared movable assets worth ₹15 crore and immovable assets valued at ₹25 lakh. Vijay is contesting the Tamil Nadu Assembly election from two constituencies: Perambur and Tiruchirappalli East. This declaration comes as election officials have seized ₹300 crore in cash and goods to ensure free and fair elections in the state. The election affidavit provides a snapshot of Vijay's financial holdings ahead of the polls.

stories.headline: US must let IAEA do its job in Iran The US-Israeli war on Iran has already destabilised the global economy and the geopolitics of the Middle East.
stories.summary: The US-Israeli conflict with Iran has destabilized the global economy and Middle East geopolitics. The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) is the legally mandated body to monitor Iran's nuclear stockpile under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation. Recent US-Israeli strikes on Iran have triggered a dangerous escalation spiral, impacting Europe's security. Iran has described US proposals to end the month-long war as "unrealistic, illogical, and excessive" and has continued to launch missiles on Israel. The IAEA's role is crucial in accounting for and monitoring Iran's nuclear activities, and it is essential that the US allows the agency to perform its duties. The ongoing conflict has significant implications for global stability and security, with the situation continuing to escalate. The IAEA's involvement is seen as a necessary step to de-escalate tensions and ensure compliance with international nuclear agreements.

stories.headline: Dow Jones, S&P, Nasdaq open higher as Trump signals progress in Iran talks US markets rise on hopes of Middle East de escalation, oil and commodities surge, Asian stocks slide, volatility stays high as investors track geopolitics
stories.summary: US stocks opened higher on Monday after President Donald Trump claimed progress in talks with Iran. The Dow Jones rose 0.4% to above 45,350, while the S&P 500 added 0.2%. The Nasdaq initially opened flat but later turned slightly negative. The market gains were driven by hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East, despite Trump's warning of possible escalation if no deal is reached on the Strait of Hormuz. Oil prices surged, topping $115, as investors tracked geopolitics. In contrast, Asian and Indian markets slid. Trump's comments on destroying key oil facilities on Kharg Island and decimating Iran's power infrastructure also contributed to the volatility. The markets remain highly volatile as investors closely monitor the developments in US-Iran talks.

stories.headline: Bitcoin Price Teeters On Iran Talks As Geopolitics And Options Flows Trap Price In Narrow Range Bitcoin price moved higher Sunday night into Monday as Iran peace negotiations continue.
stories.summary: Bitcoin price increased on Monday as Iran peace negotiations continued, with BTC reclaiming $70,000 after an Iran ceasefire report. On Monday, Bitcoin and Ethereum opened at $65,957.60 and $1,982.74, respectively, nearly even with Sunday's closing prices. Altcoins also tracked Bitcoin higher, although sentiment towards the sector remained frail amid continued risk aversion. The price movement is influenced by geopolitics and options flows, keeping the price in a narrow range. Iran's situation is being closely watched, with the country allowing passage through Hormuz for friendly countries, while the US has warned of striking Iran harder than ever if peace talks fall through. The ongoing Iran talks are impacting Bitcoin's price, with some traders using Iran's announcements as a "reverse indicator" for trading decisions. Bitcoin's price is currently volatile, reacting to developments in the Iran negotiations.

stories.headline: Bitcoin Hashrate Dips After Iran Tensions; HOOD Down 16% This Month Geopolitics and energy constraints shaped Bitcoin’s landscape in March as a notable drop in hashrate coincided with a geopolitical flare-up tied to Iran.
stories.summary: Bitcoin's hashrate dropped nearly 6% in March following a geopolitical flare-up tied to Iran. The conflict led to a $5,000 price drop, triggering $657 million in liquidations, but the cryptocurrency rebounded to around $67,000-$68,000. The hashrate decline coincided with rising US Treasury yields, which kept prices stagnant. Meanwhile, Robinhood's stock (HOOD) fell 16% this month amid declining crypto activity. The combination of geopolitical tensions and energy constraints has significantly impacted Bitcoin's landscape. The cryptocurrency's price and hashrate have been affected by the ongoing volatility, with the hashrate decline indicating a decrease in mining activity. The situation is being closely monitored as the market continues to experience complex fluctuations.

stories.headline: Role Reversal: Tamil Nadu's Triumphs, Trends & Turmoils If Tamil Nadu politics before the nineties often resembled a game of snakes and ladders played under Delhi’s shadow, the decade after 1991 altered the board
stories.summary: Tamil Nadu's political landscape underwent significant changes after 1991. The state's politics, previously influenced by Delhi, began to shift with the convergence of Tamil Nationalist organizations and Dravidian parties during the Anti Hindi Imposition Movement. The Sri Lankan civil war in 2025 led to the emergence of small Tamil militant groups in India. The AIADMK, led by Jayalalithaa, reunited in 1989 after contesting the 1989 elections as separate factions. Notably, five out of seven chief ministers from Dravidian parties had ties to Tamil cinema, including M. G. Ramachandran and J. Jayalalithaa. The Congress party's stance also fluctuated, having dismissed the DMK government in 1991, and later making shifts in its posture. These events have shaped the state's politics, with Dravidian parties and Tamil nationalism playing key roles in shaping the state's trajectory.

stories.headline: No other headlines were provided to select from, therefore only one can be returned.
stories.summary: Multiple news sources are available for readers to stay informed. Google News offers a wide range of articles, videos, and titles on various topics. APNews.com provides independent journalism from around the globe, covering breaking news, headlines, and in-depth stories. Yahoo News also offers breaking news, videos, and photos, while NPR provides audio and podcast coverage of national and world events, politics, business, and science. Additionally, CBS News delivers breaking news coverage of top headlines, focusing on balanced and trustworthy reporting. These sources allow readers to access the latest news and headlines from various perspectives and formats, keeping them up-to-date on current events and major stories. Readers can choose from these sources to stay informed on local and global news.

stories.headline: Stock market today (March 30, 2026): Which are the top gainers and losers in Nifty50 and BSE Sensex today? Check list India Business News: Equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended the last trading session of the 2025-26 financial year sharply lower on Monday, as the ongo
stories.summary: Equity benchmark indices Sensex and Nifty ended the last trading session of the 2025-26 financial year sharply lower. Nifty50 top losers included Bajaj Finance (-5.01%), Kwality Wall's (-4.79%), SBI (-3.94%), and InterGlobe Aviation (-3.81%). BSE Sensex top losers were Bajaj Finance (-5.01%) and Kwality Wall's (-4.79%), while Power Grid (0.21%) was the sole gainer. In contrast, Nifty 50 top gainers were Hindalco (+4.87%) and Coal India, along with Bharti Airtel, TCS, and Sun Pharma. The NIFTY50 index lost over 5% in the financial year ending 2025-26, while the BSE SENSEX index lost over 7% in the same period. The market closed on a low note, with notable losses in key stocks.

stories.headline: GIFT Nifty jumps 240 points on Trump's reported plans to end Iran war. Sensex, Nifty to see some relief tomorrow? Indian stock markets are poised for a potential rebound tomorrow following a significant sell-off, buoyed by positive signals from GIFT Nifty and declining oil
stories.summary: Indian stock markets are poised for a potential rebound after a significant sell-off, buoyed by positive signals from GIFT Nifty and declining oil prices. GIFT Nifty jumped 240 points to trade at 22,672, following reports of a potential de-escalation in US-Iran tensions. US President Donald Trump's announcement of a postponement of planned military strikes on Iranian power plants also contributed to the surge. The Indian stock markets were closed on Tuesday due to Shri Mahavir Jayanti, but the GIFT Nifty's 4% gain signals a potential gap-up opening. Despite this optimism, foreign investor outflows and a weakening rupee warrant caution. The Nifty50 index may regain the 23,000 levels after this positive development in the Middle East, offering relief to investors after Monday's crash. The BSE and National Stock Exchange will resume trading on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Sensex Ends FY26's Last Day 1,700 Points Low: What Led To Bloodbath? On the final day of Financial Year 2026, the Indian stock market experienced a significant decline, with the Sensex dropping 1,730 points and the Nifty falling
stories.summary: The Indian stock market ended Financial Year 2026 on a low note, with the Sensex dropping 1,730 points and the Nifty falling 523 points to 22,296. The Indian Rupee also hit a historic low of 95.22 in intra-day trade. Key factors contributing to this downturn include ongoing tensions in West Asia, rising crude oil prices, and continued foreign institutional investor outflows. Persistent foreign outflows, weak earnings, high valuations, and a weakening rupee kept sentiment subdued. Escalating tensions involving Iran further worsened the outlook by driving up energy prices and dampening hopes of US rate cuts. The significant decline resulted in investors losing approximately ₹6 lakh crore, with the Sensex and Nifty experiencing declines of 7% and 5%, respectively. The market's poor performance was attributed to a combination of global uncertainty and domestic pressures.

stories.headline: Dalal Street Bloodbath: Sensex Plunges 1,500 Pts as U.S.-Iran War Escalates; Brent Tops $115 The Indian stock market witnessed a bloodbath with the Sensex crashing 1,500 points and the Nifty 50 sliding below 22,400. The primary drivers were the escalati
stories.summary: The Indian stock market experienced a significant decline, with the Sensex plunging 1,500 points and the Nifty 50 sliding below 22,400. The S&P BSE Sensex traded at 72,142.60, down 1,471.40 points, while the NSE Nifty 50 crashed to 22,345.15, down 474.45 points. The downturn was driven by escalating US-Iran war tensions and a sharp rally in oil prices, with Brent crude topping $115. The Reserve Bank of India's new curbs on bank FX positions also contributed to the selloff. The Nifty50 ended the trading session at 22,331.40, down 488 points, and the BSE Sensex closed at 71,947.55, down 1,636 points. The rise in crude oil prices and uncertainty over the Iran war dampened investor sentiment, leading to a bearish market trend.

stories.headline: Money Is Moving Outside Traditional Systems , And Control May Be Following As digital dollar flows grow, a new layer of financial infrastructure is taking shape beyond central banks.
stories.summary: The global monetary system is undergoing a significant shift as digital dollar flows grow, leading to the emergence of a new financial infrastructure beyond central banks. Investor Ray Dalio predicts a rocky road ahead for traditional monetary systems due to debt burdens and geopolitical tensions. The US is exploring a hybrid model, where money issuance may be privatized, but control of the financial reserve strategy remains with the state. Stablecoins, denominated mostly in US dollars, could extend the dollar's reach into blockchain-based systems, allowing the US to shape digital financial infrastructure standards. Meanwhile, central banks, including China's, are exploring digital central bank money, or central bank digital currency (CBDC), raising questions about the definition of value in an interconnected system and the potential erosion of the dollar's monopoly as a benchmark of trust. This shift may impact the role of the Federal Reserve.

stories.headline: Breakfast briefing: Q1-2025 ends in a mess US struggles to smooth over Trump mistakes; enormous China current account surplus; China now dominates aluminium markets; air cargo activity surges; UST 10yr a
stories.summary: The US is struggling to recover from mistakes made during the Trump administration, while China has reported a significant current account surplus. China now dominates the aluminium market and has retaliated against the latest round of US tariffs with sweeping measures, escalating tensions and triggering market turmoil. The retaliation led to a 9.7% drop in Taiwan's Taiex on Monday. Meanwhile, air cargo activity has surged, and the US 10-year treasury yield is at 4.44%. Gold prices have dipped, while oil prices have risen. The New Zealand dollar is currently trading at 57.5 US cents, with the Trade Weighted Index at 61.4. Global trade tensions continue to rise, with the US and China engaging in anti-trade probes and countries implementing fuel affordability measures amid a growing diesel crisis.

stories.headline: Xiao Liu Advances GenAI Optimization Through Real-Time Feedback Acceleration for Enhanced User Interaction A real-time feedback optimization framework enhances GenAI chatbot performance through low-latency data transmission, stronger semantic understanding, and light
stories.summary: Xiao Liu has developed a real-time feedback optimization framework to enhance GenAI chatbot performance. The framework utilizes low-latency data transmission, allowing for stronger semantic understanding and improved user interaction. This advancement enables GenAI chatbots to process and respond to user input more efficiently, providing a more seamless and intuitive experience. The optimization framework is designed to accelerate feedback loops, enabling chatbots to learn and adapt in real-time. By leveraging this technology, GenAI chatbots can better comprehend user intent and generate more accurate and relevant responses, ultimately leading to enhanced user engagement and satisfaction. The development of this framework has the potential to significantly improve the performance and effectiveness of GenAI chatbots in various applications.

stories.headline: ST Engineering iDirect Appoints Sridhar Kuppanna as Chief Executive Officer /PRNewswire/ -- ST Engineering iDirect, a global leader in satellite communications, today announced the appointment of Sridhar Kuppanna as Chief Executive...
stories.summary: ST Engineering iDirect, a global leader in satellite communications, has appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as Chief Executive Officer. As CEO, Kuppanna will oversee the execution of the company's strategy, including its Intuition-led modernization strategy. The appointment was announced on March 30, 2026. ST Engineering iDirect is a prominent player in the satellite communications industry, and Kuppanna's leadership is expected to continue the company's growth and development. The company's strategy focuses on modernization and innovation, and Kuppanna's role will be crucial in driving this forward. With his appointment, ST Engineering iDirect aims to maintain its position as a global leader in satellite communications, providing innovative solutions to its customers. The company's announcement marks a significant development in its leadership and strategic direction.

stories.headline: Nvidia’s Jensen Huang says ‘we’ve achieved AGI.’ But no one can agree on what that means Why the most important term in tech remains hotly debated.
stories.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang claims that artificial general intelligence (AGI) has already been achieved, sparking a debate on what "general intelligence" truly means. Recent research papers contradict this statement, proposing new ways to measure AGI. Experts are divided on what qualifies as human-level artificial intelligence, with some introducing softer terminology to manage expectations and reduce regulatory scrutiny. Huang's claim has reignited the debate, highlighting the ongoing discussion on the definition and measurement of AGI. The tech industry has been retreating from the AGI label in recent months, making Huang's enthusiasm notable. The lack of consensus on AGI's definition has led to a reality check, with experts continuing to propose and debate various criteria for achieving true artificial general intelligence.

stories.headline: Ex-OpenAI's Kass: AI Is Going to Make a Lot of Winners Former OpenAI Head of Go-To-Market Zack Kass believes that while it's still early days for artificial intelligence investments, AI is going to make a lot of win
stories.summary: Former OpenAI Head of Go-To-Market Zack Kass believes artificial intelligence (AI) will create numerous winners, despite it being early days for AI investments. Kass, a sought-after voice on harnessing AI technology, shared his insights at the Citi Macro Conference in Hong Kong. Meanwhile, ex-OpenAI pioneer Ilya Sutskever warns that as AI self-improves, its trajectory may become unpredictable. OpenAI's AI tools have seen a surge in website traffic, outpacing Google and with DeepSeek closing in. The rapid development of AI has sparked debate, with optimists anticipating economic benefits and pessimists concerned about job losses. OpenAI has also shut down its AI video generator Sora, highlighting the fast-paced and unpredictable nature of the technology. As AI continues to evolve, its impact on the workplace and economy remains a topic of discussion.

stories.headline: No other headlines provided to choose from.
stories.summary: The LA Metro is planning a new line through West Hollywood, which is nearing its final stages. Meanwhile, the US State Department is updating its travel advisories ahead of April travel, amid ongoing international conflicts, including the war in Iran. The latest list of travel advisories has been released. In other news, various international news outlets, such as CNN, USNews, The Straits Times, and UrduPoint, are providing updates on national and international news, including politics, entertainment, and sports. These outlets are offering the latest news and breaking stories from around the world, with UrduPoint providing round-the-clock updates in Urdu. The news landscape is constantly evolving, with new developments emerging daily.

stories.headline: No other headlines provided to choose from.
stories.summary: Three people died when an unfinished church building collapsed in Ghana. In other news, the US has resumed embassy operations in Venezuela after a 7-year absence. Meanwhile, Spain has closed its airspace to potential US military action against Iran. These developments mark significant updates in global events. The US embassy reopening in Venezuela aims to strengthen diplomatic ties between the two countries. The closure of Spanish airspace is a response to escalating tensions between the US and Iran. These stories are part of ongoing global events, with more updates expected as situations unfold. International news outlets continue to provide real-time coverage of these and other breaking stories worldwide.
